overview.title: Pocket MP3 Player
overview.description: A slim, portable MP3 player powered by an ESP32 audio stack with local storage, a color display, physical playback controls, USB-C charging, Bluetooth-ready connectivity, and a 3D printed rounded enclosure.

requirements: Play MP3 files from expandable microSD storage.
Display track, battery, and playback status on a compact color screen.
Use physical play/pause, next, and previous buttons.
Charge a compact Li-Po battery through USB-C.
requirements.power_needs: 3.7V Li-Po battery with USB-C charge management and protected 3.3V logic rail.
safety_notes: Verify Li-Po polarity before connecting USB power.
Keep amplifier outputs isolated from logic GPIO pins.

components.name: ESP32-WROOM-32E Module
components.rationale: Main Controller. Provides Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity for app and web interface.

components.name: 8 Ohm 1W Mini Speaker
components.rationale: Small Internal Speaker. Small 8 Ohm 1W speaker for audio playback.

components.name: 3.7V 500mAh Li-Po Battery
components.rationale: Rechargeable Power Source. Small battery for the wireless audio module.

components.name: TP4056 USB-C Li-Ion Charger Module
components.rationale: Li-Po Charging & Power Management. Charges the Li-ion battery and provides overcharge/discharge protection.

components.name: Audio DAC with Amplifier
components.rationale: Digital-to-analog audio conversion and speaker drive.

components.name: Track Information Display
components.rationale: Color display for waveform, battery, and track metadata.

components.name: OLED Display Bezel Mount
components.rationale: Aligns display behind the front window.

assembly.title: Print and prepare enclosure
assembly.description: Print the rounded main shell, front bezel, back cover with speaker vents, OLED mount, and ESP32 board mount.

assembly.title: Assemble power subsystem
assembly.description: Connect the 3.7V Li-Po battery to the TP4056 charger module and route protected output to the ESP32, display, DAC, and microSD module.
assembly.danger_message: Verify battery polarity before applying USB-C power.

assembly.title: Wire audio, display, and storage
assembly.description: Wire the display over I2C, the microSD module over SPI, and the DAC amplifier over I2S. Keep speaker wiring away from ESP32 antenna edge.

assembly.title: Install controls and close shell
assembly.description: Mount tactile switches behind the front control pad, press-fit the button caps, install heat-set inserts, and close with M2 screws.

mechanical.mounting_guidance: Use internal bosses for ESP32, DAC, charger, microSD module, OLED bezel, speaker, and battery tray.
fabrication_details: Heat-set M2 inserts for a serviceable rear cover.
Speaker vent slots on the side or back cover.

cad_sources.name: Adafruit Walkmp3rson CAD files
cad_sources.license: Adafruit Learn reference design; verify reuse terms on source page
cad_sources.notes: Use as a portable audio enclosure reference and adapt cutouts for ESP32, OLED, USB-C, buttons, and speaker.

cad_sources.name: ESP32 Project Enclosure with OLED SSD1306
cad_sources.license: Creative Commons attribution-sharealike listing mirror; verify original Printables license
cad_sources.notes: Good reference for ESP32/OLED internal mounting and display bezel geometry.

spatial_relationships.relation: display above controller on the front face
spatial_relationships.notes: Display sits above the controller and forward on Y.
OLED centerline is held by the printed mount.
Battery and charger remain on the lower internal plane.
Amplifier output is routed upward to the rear speaker.
Button cap protrudes through the front bezel.
Button cap protrudes through the front bezel.
Button cap protrudes through the front bezel.

fabrication_notes: Use heat-set inserts for repeatable assembly.
